about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Curious about ideas, people and the world. This is where I collect what I learn along the way.
bio: I am a recent graduate of IIT Kanpur with a curiosity that extends far beyond any single field. While technology remains one of my primary interests, I am equally drawn to books, music, philosophy, and the ideas that shape how we understand ourselves and the world.

I enjoy building systems, solving difficult problems, and learning how complex things work. Over the years, that curiosity has led me through software, machine learning, robotics, writing, and music. I have played guitar for several years and continue to find joy in both playing and listening to music.

This website is my personal archive, a collection of books that influenced me, music that stayed with me, and thoughts worth preserving. It is a small record of what I have learned and what I am still trying to understand.

contact.title: Let's Connect
contact.subtitle: Always happy to discuss ideas, books, music, technology, or anything interesting.
intro: If something on this website resonates with you, feel free to reach out.
I'm particularly interested in conversations around technology, books, philosophy, writing and music.

hero.name: Yuval Bansal
hero.title: Engineer. Reader. Musician. Writer.
hero.description: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.

currentFocus.title: Current Focus
currentFocus.subtitle: A snapshot of what occupies my attention these days.

timelineSection.title: Journey
timelineSection.subtitle: A few milestones that shaped who I am.

timeline.title: Graduation from IIT Kanpur
timeline.description: Completed my degree while continuing to explore software, AI, books and music.

timeline.title: Software Development Internship, Assisting Doctors In Indore
timeline.description: Developed software solutions to ease clinical workflows at healthcare facilities in Indore.

timeline.title: Inter IIT Tech Meet 14.0, IIT Patna
timeline.description: Worked on building a fully autonomous warehouse robot, winning a bronze medal while representing IIT Kanpur.

timeline.title: Institute Secretary, Science And Technology Council, IIT Kanpur
timeline.description: Elected to lead and manage student activities across all technical domains at IIT Kanpur.

timeline.title: Data Engineering Internship, Semusi Technologies
timeline.description: Built ETL pipelines, machine learning systems, and production-ready data solutions.

timeline.title: Inter IIT Tech Meet 13.0, IIT Bombay
timeline.description: Led the simulation of an HVAC system and earned a bronze medal while representing IIT Kanpur.

timeline.title: Coordinator, Robotics Club, IIT Kanpur
timeline.description: Led projects and teams in competitions including WRC, GujCOST Robofest 4.0, Techkriti, and Techfest.

timeline.title: Simulations Intern, Energy Internet Corporation
timeline.description: Worked on simulation-driven engineering solutions and analytical workflows.

timeline.title: Technical Organizer, WCEAM 2024
timeline.description: Managed conference operations, developed the website, and automated organizational workflows.

timeline.title: Inter IIT Tech Meet 12.0, IIT Madras
timeline.description: Contributed to the mechanical design and modelling of an industrial agitator while representing IIT Kanpur.

timeline.title: Started at IIT Kanpur
timeline.description: Began my journey in Mechanical Engineering while exploring programming, robotics, and technology.

quotes.quote: The unexamined life is not worth living.
quotes.author: Socrates

quotes.quote: Amor fati: let that be my love from henceforth!
quotes.author: Nietzsche

quotes.quote: Do unto others as you would have others do unto you.
quotes.author: Jesus Christ

quotes.quote: It is the hour to be drunken! To escape being the martyred slaves of time, be ceaselessly drunk. On wine, on poetry, or on virtue, as you wish.
quotes.author: Charles Baudelaire

quotes.quote: Happiness is when what you think, what you say, and what you do are in harmony.
quotes.author: Mahatma Gandhi

quotes.quote: Until you make the unconscious conscious, it will direct your life and you will call it fate.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.quote: The best is the enemy of the good.
quotes.author: Voltaire

quotes.quote: There is nothing either good or bad, but thinking makes it so.
quotes.author: William Shakespeare

musicPage.title: Soundtrack
musicPage.subtitle: Music that has accompanied different chapters of my life.
musicPage.description: From classic rock and blues to folk and pop, these are the artists whose work continues to inspire me.

artists.name: The Beatles
artists.note: Timeless songwriting, constant reinvention, and a catalogue that continues to influence generations of musicians.

artists.name: Pink Floyd
artists.note: Immersive albums that blend music, philosophy, and storytelling into unforgettable journeys.

artists.name: Bob Dylan
artists.note: Poetic lyricism and storytelling that blur the line between music and literature.

artists.name: John Mayer
artists.note: A rare blend of lyrical introspection and expressive guitar playing. His music balances technical brilliance with emotional honesty.

artists.name: A. R. Rahman
artists.note: A composer whose music blends tradition and innovation with remarkable ease. His soundtracks are rich in emotion, atmosphere, and unforgettable melodies.

artists.name: Led Zeppelin
artists.note: Powerful riffs, dynamic songwriting, and legendary musicianship. Their music laid the foundation for much of modern rock.

artists.name: Nirvana
artists.note: Raw, honest, and unapologetically human. Nirvana captured a generation's frustrations while creating some of the most iconic rock music ever recorded.

artists.name: Amit Trivedi
artists.note: Known for constantly reinventing his sound, Amit Trivedi brings together folk, rock, classical, and contemporary influences with remarkable creativity.

artists.name: Radiohead
artists.note: Restless experimentation and deeply introspective songwriting that rewards repeated listening.

booksPage.title: Library
booksPage.subtitle: Books that shaped how I think.
booksPage.description: A collection of books that influenced my ideas, challenged my assumptions and expanded my perspective.

books.title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
books.author: Robert Kiyosaki
books.note: Introduces fundamental ideas about money, investing, and financial independence.

books.title: How To Win Friends And Influence People
books.author: Dale Carnegie
books.note: A timeless guide to communication, relationships, and understanding people.

books.title: The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari
books.author: Robin Sharma
books.note: A story-driven exploration of personal growth, balance, and fulfillment.

books.title: The Story of My Experiments with Truth
books.author: Mahatma Gandhi
books.note: A candid account of Gandhi's life, values, and pursuit of truth.

books.title: Man's Search for Meaning
books.author: Viktor E. Frankl
books.note: A profound reflection on suffering, resilience, and the search for purpose.

books.title: Ikigai: The Japanese Secret to a Long and Happy Life
books.author: Francesc Miralles & Hector Garcia
books.note: An introduction to the Japanese concept of finding purpose and meaning in life.

books.title: Thirteen Reasons Why
books.author: Jay Asher
books.note: A young adult novel exploring relationships, choices, and consequences.

books.title: The INFJ Personality Guide
books.author: Bo Miller
books.note: An overview of the INFJ personality type and its characteristics.

books.title: Bhagavad Gita Bhashya
books.author: Gopalanand Swami
books.note: A commentary on the Bhagavad Gita offering spiritual and philosophical insights.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.note: A practical framework for building good habits and breaking bad ones.

books.title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
books.author: Mark Manson
books.note: A counterintuitive approach to prioritizing what truly matters.

books.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.note: A sweeping exploration of human history, from hunter-gatherers to modern civilizations.

books.title: Why I Am an Atheist
books.author: Bhagat Singh
books.note: A thoughtful essay examining reason, belief, and intellectual independence.

books.title: Metamorphosis
books.author: Franz Kafka
books.note: A surreal and unsettling exploration of alienation and identity.

books.title: The Psychology of Money
books.author: Morgan Housel
books.note: Explores how human behavior shapes financial decisions more than technical knowledge.

books.title: The Stranger
books.author: Albert Camus
books.note: A landmark existential novel exploring absurdity, freedom, and meaning.

books.title: White Nights
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.note: A tender and melancholic story of loneliness, hope, and fleeting connection.

books.title: Active Listening
books.author: Carl Rogers & Richard Farson
books.note: A concise exploration of listening as a powerful tool for understanding and communication.

books.title: Zero To One
books.author: Peter Thiel & Blake Masters
books.note: An argument for creating unique value by building things that do not yet exist.

books.title: Animal Farm
books.author: George Orwell
books.note: A sharp and timeless satire on power, corruption, and the ways ideals can be distorted by those who wield authority.

thoughtsPage.title: Thoughts
thoughtsPage.subtitle: Poems, journals and ideas.
thoughtsPage.description: Some writings are records of moments. Some are attempts to understand the world. Others are simply questions.
